title: aloysia gratissima var. chacoensis
intro: Aloysia gratissima var. chacoensis, also known as Chaco albahaca or Chaco sweet basil, is a variety of the Aloysia gratissima plant, a type of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Aloysia gratissima var. chacoensis is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
